body:
{
  "title": "Piper Bergmann",
  "compiled_truth": "Piper Bergmann is a Partner at [Prism Ventures](companies/prism-ventures), where she leads the firm's data systems and infrastructure practice. She joined Prism in 2023 after a stint at a mid-stage growth fund where she built a reputation for being an unusually sharp pattern matcher — the kind of investor who can read a technical architecture doc and spot the structural bet buried three layers deep. Her analytical temperament is well known among founders; she doesn't waste time on narrative and will drill into benchmarks before asking about market size.

Bergmann's portfolio at Prism is concentrated around deterministic and low-level systems companies. She led Prism's investment in [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) during its seed round in early 2026, a deal that reportedly closed fast after a single deep-dive session with Anchor's founding team. Colleagues say she was convinced within an hour that single-pass primitives would reshape how data pipelines are built, and she pushed internally to preempt the round. Her conviction on Anchor has only deepened over time — she's been spotted at multiple company offsites and occassionally drops into engineering standups, though she's careful not to overstep.

Outside of Anchor, Piper sits on the board of [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay), which builds edge relay infrastructure, and is an advisor to [Prism Protocol](companies/prism-protocol), a separate entity from Prism Ventures that focuses on open data standards. She's been vocal in private conversations about the convergence between deterministic data handling and edge compute — a thesis she's been refining since at least 2025.

Personally, Bergmann is known as someone who prefers small dinners to large conferences, though she made an exception to speak at InfraWeek 2027 in a panel on funding deep-tech infrastructure. She's terse in Slack, generous in 1:1s, and has a habit of sending founders papers from the '90s on compression theory with the note "worth re-reading." Her style isn't flashy, but founders in her portfolio consistently describe her as the most technically engaged board member they've worked with.

She studied applied mathematics at a small liberal arts college in the northeast before pivoting to venture through an operating role at a now-defunct data analytics startup. That operating background colors her approach — she thinks in systems, not decks.",
  "timeline": "- **2026-02-14** | Piper leads [Prism Ventures](companies/prism-ventures) seed investment in [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor); deal closes in under two weeks.
- **2026-05-09** | Joins the board of [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay) following their Series A close.
- **2026-08-22** | Hosts a private dinner in San Francisco for infrastructure founders, including several from the Anchor team.
- **2026-11-03** | Advises [Prism Protocol](companies/prism-protocol) on their open data standards working group charter.
- **2027-01-18** | Attends [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) Q1 offsite; participates in architecture review session on single-pass pipeline performance.
- **2027-04-27** | Speaks at InfraWeek 2027 on a panel titled "Who Funds the Plumbing? Deep-Tech Infra in a Hype Market."
- **2027-09-12** | Co-authors internal Prism Ventures memo on convergence between edge relay and deterministic data layers, referencing [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay) and [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor).
- **2028-01-30** | Participates in [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) Series A due diligence as existing investor; signals strong pro-rata intent.
- **2028-05-15** | Named to a trade publication's "40 Under 40 in Infrastructure Investing" list."
}
